steps.title: Identify the angle at P using collinearity
steps.content:
The marked angle at P is 60°. Since M, P, and Q lie on the same line (diagonal $\overline{MQ}$), the angles on either side of this line at P are supplementary.

The marked angle appears to be $\angle QPR = 60°$, so the supplementary angle on the other side is $\angle MPR = 180° - 60° = 120°$.

steps.title: Apply isosceles triangle properties to triangle PMR
steps.content: Triangle PMR has $MP = PR$ (given), making it isosceles. With the vertex angle $\angle MPR = 120°$, the two base angles must be equal. Since the angles in a triangle sum to 180°: $$\angle PMR + \angle PRM + 120° = 180°$$ $$\angle PMR + \angle PRM = 60°$$ $$\angle PMR = \angle PRM = 30°$$

steps.title: Find ∠QMR using point collinearity
steps.content:
Since P lies on segment $\overline{MQ}$ (between M and Q), the ray from M through P points directly toward Q.

Therefore, $\angle QMR$ and $\angle PMR$ are the same angle (the angle at M measured from the line MQ to the line MR).

Thus $\angle QMR = \angle PMR = 30°$. That matches a grid answer of 30.
